transcript.text:
-How much was a bowl of Jjajangmyun when you first started? -500 Won (~$0.39 USD)
[Yes... Let's go back to that era...]
[Same taste as 40 years ago]
Places like this are the best
[Let's get started]
I get that you're a big eater, but still...
[Never seen anything like this in 40 years, right?]
[I'm sorry for doubting you...]
Hello everyone
I'm here at Jegi-dong today
I heard there's a hidden gem here
And they barely let anyone film
So I think I'm the first YouTuber
Not even that many reviews online
But they've been here for 40 years
[Hong Kong Joonghwa Yori] [Seoul, Dongdaemun-gu]

[Down the stairs]
What's this?
[A small restaurant in the basement]
-Hello -Yes, welcome
Hello!
This is that delivery box
It's been so long since I've seen one of these
[A place that froze time 40 years ago]
Look at the menu
All written It's going to be great
Do you have kimchi fried rice?
Yes, over there
There's a menu here too
A hidden menu
Naengmyun... (cold buckwheat noodles)
Well then,

1 Jjajamgyun,
1 stir-fried Jjajangmyun,
1 Jjamppong,
1 kimchi fried rice,
1 tangsuyuk,
1 Nanja Wans, please
Well... Are you eating by yourself?
Looks great! Feels like I haven't had it in a while
Gotta pour the sauce
I like pouring this sauce in too
Bon appetit!
What? Delicious!
Tangsuyuk is usually crispy and very hard on the outside
It's not like that at all It's very soft
I didn't even need to pour the sauce in
I pour it in to get the pieces soft and soggy

But these are very soft and tender already
This is exactly what I like
Jjajangmyun comes with cucumbers (she didn't get it of course)
Boiled egg here too
Interesting
Let's mix it up
Tangsuyuk tastes so good Jjajangmyun should be good too
I can smell the strong jjajang sauce The old traditional way
Tastes just like something I had as a kid
The flavors are not too strong
The sauce tastes great
Delicious
It's not overwhelming
Great for children
Really good
Stir-fried jjajangmyun This one seemed popular

There aren't too many reviews online
But every one of them said this place is great
A real hole-in-the-wall place!
[Let's pour the sauce in]
Not the whole thing though
Mix this up
It already looks so good
I usually can't eat these because of the big onion chunks
But onions in here are pretty small
Let's give it a try
[Slap]
There aren't many times that I like eating stir-fried jjajangmyun
I just prefer the regular one over the greasy one
It's very rare that I like it stir-fried
These...
Tasty in a new way

The regular customer from earlier highly recommended the Jjamppong
Their Jjamppong here is unrivaled
I'm being serious
I know this flavor!
[But what was it...?]
Not too strong
But really good
Relaxes my stomach
Nanja Wans Let's try 1
These are all handmade
Amazing
Chewy
Some kimchi fried rice too
I've never seen kimchi fried rice served like this before
Usually stir-fried together
But this is served over rice

[How is it?]
It tastes really good
Thank you
So good
This is going to be great too
When it comes to sauces over rice, other places give less sauce and more rice
But this is served at a perfect ratio
[Well...?]
[Her face says it all]
[So good at eating]
So good
Good thing Tangsuyuk is size Small
I should've ordered a Large
Kimchi fried rice is really different and tasty
Potato here too
I love having potatoes in Jajjangmyun

Not many places that do that now
Delicious
Their sauce is tasty
Mussel Jjamppong flavor
It's like those Jjamppong sold at street markets
Mix the sauce up
I love soft Tangsuyuk
No hard Tangsuyuk
Excuse me,
Do you do delivery?
We do
Are you on the Baemin delivery app?
No
So just phone?
We don't even advertise our restaurant
So only for those who know us

Can I please have your number?
We only do it local No big roads
Only in walking distance?
How about (her address)?
No way!
When we get a customer dining in,
they are our priority, not delivery
We try to focus on our regulars over anything
So just regulars
That's a bummer
Soft-fried egg
I should've broken this up earlier
[It's never too late!]
It tastes even better with the fried egg
1 bite of this too
I just ate a big piece of meat

[Scrape everything up]
-How much was a bowl of Jjajangmyun when you first started? -500 Won (~$0.39 USD)
That's the price 40 years ago?
Now it's 6,000 Won
Over 10x more expensive now
It has big chunks of meat
But the restaurant feels very old
[You can see the age from the interior]
The clocks are incredible
It's like I'm at my grandma's
Fried rice, fried rice
That was great
[Great job!]
She finished it
[She really did...]
It was very delicious

Thank you for enjoying our food
You scraped everything up
Amazing
Thanks everyone!
Hello?
Do you know Tzuyang?
No, right?
